# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

```dockerfile
# Use an official Python runtime as a parent image
FROM python:latest

# Set environment variables to be used by any commands within this Dockerfile 
ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE 1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED 1\
    
# Increase the default file descriptor limit (this allows us not have write permission errors)
ENV MAX_FD_COUNT=65535  
RUN ulimit -n ${MAX_FD_COUNT} && find /dev/ | xargs chown your-user:your-group  # Required to be able change file descriptor limit of users. Replace 'your-user' and 'your-group'.

# Install Python dependencies (you can add more in here)  
RUN pip install --no-cache numpy pandas scikit-learn flask psycopg2 redis==3.*  # Required libraries version, you may change it. Use * to have all latest versions available if needed on your project; also replace '*' by a specific minor/version number as required
# Install NodeJS and NPM for UI development (uncomment these lines below)   
RUN apt-get update && apt install -y nodejs npm   # you may need to specify version 14 or newer depending on your setup; Replace 'node' with the name of a different environment if necessary. Also change line above according to docker file user permissions set in Dockerfile section below this comment
WORKDIR /usr/src/app
COPY package*.json ./    # Assumes that you have NodeJS and NPM already installed  on your base image, make sure the directories match up with them if they are not. Also replace 'package' by a specific minor version or major as required (you can use node vX for example) when running Dockerfile in another environment
RUN npm install    # Install Node dependencies inside this container using NPM  you may need to specify what packages/modules your application needs, these should be installed with `npm i --save` command. Replace 'package' by a specific minor version or major as required (you can use node vX for example)
EXPOSE 80    #Expose port used on the host machine where Docker is running from this service and other services inside container, replace ports with your own ones if needed unless they are default. Use only those that you want to expose or leave empty as it will use all available unexposed docker network interface
EXPOSE 8080    #Expose port used on the host machine where Docker is running from this service and other services inside container, replace ports with your own ones if needed unless they are default. Use only those that you want to expose or leave empty as it will use all available unexposed docker network interface
EXPOSE 5432    #Expose port used on the host machine where Docker is running from this service and other services inside container, replace ports with your own ones if needed unless they are default. Use only those that you want to expose or leave empty as it will use all available unexposed docker network interface
EXPOSE 6379    #Expose port used on the host machine where Docker is running from this service and other services inside container, replace ports with your own ones if needed unless they are default.
